text:
## Overview
Redwood Dedicated is being formalized with a throughput SLO that customers can purchase, monitor, and enforce via standardized tiers. This PRD defines:

- The customer problem and the Dedicated throughput commitment we will offer
- The canonical metric and measurement rules (what counts, what does not)
- Tier taxonomy (Standard / Plus / Premium) with explicit entitlements and exclusions
- Mapping from tier → standardized hardware “shapes” (by region) and packaging guardrails
- Rollout plan, launch criteria, and success metrics

This document is customer-commitment–shaping. Engineering implementation details live in the SRE SLO doc and Infra shape catalog.

---

## Why now
Dedicated demand has shifted from “isolated capacity” to “predictable capacity.” Enterprise and high-volume customers are requesting:

- A written throughput guarantee that can be referenced in procuremen...

title: PRD: Hosted Data Residency Enforcement (Region Pinning + Request-Time Blocking)

text:
## Summary
Hosted data residency enforcement adds customer-configurable **region pinning** for Redwood Hosted API and enforces that pinning at request time via deterministic checks.

This PRD defines:
- The customer problem and target personas (EU regulated + Enterprise platform teams)
- Policy model requirements (allowed region set + primary region; optional API key overrides)
- Enforcement requirements across routing/fallback, background work, logging/observability, and operational tooling
- Public error contract requirements (consistent HTTP status + machine-readable code + actionable guidance)
- Auditability requirements (decision logs + audit log events for config changes)
- Success metrics and phased rollout plan

## Problem statement
Enterprise customers increasingly require guarantees that **customer content** (prompts, uploaded inputs, tool outputs, model outputs, embeddings ...

title: Design Spec: Residency Policy Model + Admin UX (Hosted Data Residency Enforcement)

text:
h1. Overview

This document specifies:
* The *residency policy object model* (Org / Project / API key scopes), including precedence and validation.
* The *Console admin UX* to configure and view policies, including change history.
* The *“blocked requests” troubleshooting* UI for admins (and internal support) to diagnose enforcement blocks.

This spec supports the "Hosted data residency enforcement" initiative: customers can pin hosted processing to an allowed region set (e.g., EU-only), and the system deterministically blocks requests that would violate the configured policy.

h2. Goals

* Provide a clear, minimal policy model that matches enterprise expectations (org-level defaults + narrower overrides).
* Make it hard to misconfigure: strong validation, clear impact warnings, and safe defaults.
* Make enforcement outcomes observable: recent blocks, request IDs, and actionable next ...

title: Dedicated Burst Capacity Option — Pricing & Packaging Proposal (v1)
preview:
## Summary
Dedicated Burst Capacity (“Burst”) is an add-on for Redwood Dedicated customers that allows temporary, controlled throughput above their reserved baseline capacity by borrowing GPUs from Redwood-managed shared burst pools (per region and hardware class). text:
## Summary
Dedicated Burst Capacity (“Burst”) is an add-on for Redwood Dedicated customers that allows temporary, controlled throughput above their reserved baseline capacity by borrowing GPUs from Redwood-managed shared burst pools (per region and hardware class). Burst is designed to cover predictable peaks (launches, batch jobs, indexing windows) without requiring permanent over-provisioning.

This document proposes:
- Packaging tiers and eligibility
- Default limits/guardrails by tier
- Metering and billing line items
- Sales + Customer Success guidance (when to recommend, how to set expectations)
- Contract/SLA positioning (what is and is not guaranteed)

## Target customer outcomes (what we’re selling)
Customers want:
- Higher throughput for limited time windows without resizing baseline reservations
- Clear limits they can plan around (caps, cooldowns, predictable denial behavi...

text:
h1. Internal Unit Economics Dashboard (v1) — Product Requirements Document

h2. Summary
Redwood Inference will build an internal-only “Unit Economics” dashboard in the Redwood Console to make inference cost drivers visible and actionable across Product, Engineering, and Finance.

The dashboard’s primary KPI is *Blended $/1M output tokens*, with drilldowns by:
* Model (including model version and quantization profile)
* Region (canonical normalized region)
* Customer tier (Self-serve / Dedicated / Private / Enterprise)

The dashboard is intended to support Cost Optimization Automation efforts (batching, caching, quantization) by:
1) quantifying impact, 2) identifying regressions, and 3) enabling prioritization of runtime/infra work.

h2. Goals (v1)
* Establish a *trusted, finance-aligned* definition of “Blended $/1M output tokens” that reconciles with infra spend inputs and platform me...

title: Hosted API graceful degradation: automatic fallback routing
preview:
# Overview
This document specifies how Redwood Hosted API will **automatically fall back** during partial outages and capacity shortfalls. The goal is to reduce customer-visible errors/timeouts without violating contract constraints (data residency, pinning) or feature constraints (tool calling, structured output).
...
text:
# Overview
This document specifies how Redwood Hosted API will **automatically fall back** during partial outages and capacity shortfalls. The goal is to reduce customer-visible errors/timeouts without violating contract constraints (data residency, pinning) or feature constraints (tool calling, structured output).

This is a *completeness* spec for all forms of Hosted API auto-fallback.

# User-facing behavior
When a request cannot be served on the primary route, the router will select a fallback route and proceed without requiring retries from the customer. Customers may observe:
- Same response schema as the primary route (OpenAI-compatible endpoints included).
- Potentially different latency characteristics.
- Optional response header `x-redwood-fallback: <reason_code>` in v2 (behind feature flag).

# In-scope endpoints
- `POST /v1/chat/completions` (streaming + non-streaming)
- `...

title: PRD: Dedicated Burst Capacity (Controlled Burst Option)
preview:
h1. Summary

Bursting is strictly controlled via admiss...
text:
h1. Summary

Dedicated Burst Capacity is an add-on option for Redwood Dedicated customers that allows *temporary throughput above a tenant’s reserved (baseline) GPU allocation* by borrowing from Redwood-managed *burst pools* (shared, segmented by region and hardware class). Bursting is strictly controlled via admission control, fairness enforcement, cooldown windows, and fleet-wide circuit breakers to prevent noisy-neighbor effects and protect platform-wide SLOs.

This PRD defines the customer problem, user stories, requirements, non-goals, SLOs, pricing assumptions, rollout plan, and success metrics.

h1. Problem statement

Dedicated customers routinely experience predictable peaks (e.g., batch re-indexing, weekly traffic spikes, product launches). Today, they must either:

* Over-provision baseline Dedicated capacity (wasting cost most of the time), or
* Accept degraded performance ...
